author: e-ddykim
title: RISC-V Build fails - artifact upload FinalizeArtifact returns 403 Forbidden
signature: Failed to FinalizeArtifact non-retryable error 403 Forbidden|Infrastructure

failed_jobs.name: Build (RVV 1.0)
failed_jobs.symptom: Upload openvino tests package step fails: FinalizeArtifact HTTP 403 Forbidden from blob storage intermediary

key_errors: ##[error]Failed to FinalizeArtifact: Received non-retryable error: Failed request: (403) Forbidden: Error from intermediary with HTTP status code 403 "Forbidden"
root_cause: The build and all compilation steps completed successfully. The failure occurred in the 'Upload openvino tests package' artifact upload step during the FinalizeArtifact call, which received a 403 Forbidden response from the blob storage intermediary (Azure Blob Storage). This is a transient infrastructure issue with the GitHub Actions artifact storage service, not related to the PR changes. The 'Upload openvino package' (the preceding upload) succeeded; only the second larger upload (openvino_tests, ~120MB) hit the 403.
reproduction_steps: N/A - transient infrastructure failure not reproducible locally.
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed Build (RVV 1.0) job to retry the artifact upload.
If the failure recurs, escalate to GitHub Actions support as the artifact storage service is returning 403 errors.
prevention_strategies: This is a transient GitHub Actions artifact storage service failure. No code change can prevent it. Consider adding retry logic around artifact upload steps if this becomes frequent.
historical_context: Similar infrastructure artifact upload failures have been seen before (e.g., investigation 2026-07-14-13-52-06-309-29334800771 for debian packages upload). These are typically transient and resolved by re-running the job.
